rulings.Case Title: FUND RECOVERY SERVICES, INC., A CA. CORP. VS. LIBERT MYERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Injunctive And Declatory Relief; Notice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 14, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT LIBERT MYERS' Motion For Injunctive And Declaratory Relief. The motion is granted pursuant to CCP 697.410(c). The moving party (Libert Myers Sr.) establishes that he is not the judgment debtor and the judgment was entered against his son (Libert Myers Jr.). The collection efforts against the father are improper and he is entitled to relief. Plaintiff shall prepare and deliver a recordable document releasing the liens to moving party's attorney within ten days from entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JULES RADCLIFFE et al VS. PHUONG PHAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defts To Respond To Disovery And To Produce Additional Documents Pursuant To Discovery Requests And Requests For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 14, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF JULES RADCLIFFE, MICHAEL JELLISON Motion To Compel Defendants To Respond To Discovery And To Produce Additional Documents Pursuant To Discovery Requests And Requests For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald notes that he is not related to any person at Greenstein & McDonald. Prior to the hearing, the parties are directed to engage in one final meet and confer session to attempt to narrow the issues that are raised in the present motion. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA REBOLLEDO et al VS. UNION BANK, NA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 14, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT MUFG UNION BANK, N.A. F/K/A UNION BANK, N.A. Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part; denied in part. Plaintiffs provided responses to the outstanding interrogatories after service of the motion to compel. While no objections were made, the Court finds that the responses to Interrogatory Nos. 4, 8, 9 and 16 are incomplete. Plaintiffs shall provide adequate, verified supplemental responses to these interrogatories no later than November 24, 2014. Plaintiffs' responses to the other interrogatories are sufficient. While Plaintiffs' failure to respond to this discovery may have be inadvertent, sanctions in the amount of $750 are appropriate given that responses were not served until three weeks after this motion was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: COX, CASTLE & NICHOLSON et al VS. ACCO ENGINEERED SYSTEMS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay, Or, Alternatively, Deny Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 14, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFFS COX, CASTLE & NICHOLSON, VIGILANT INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Stay Or, Alternatively, Deny Arbitration. Motion granted. Because the parties did not expressly agree to the procedural provisions of the Federal Arbitration Act, the procedural provisions of the California Arbitration Act-including CCP section 1281.2(c)-apply. Pursuant to CCP section 1281.2(c), the arbitration proceedings are stayed pending resolution of the litigation given the risk of conflicting rulings that could result if the proceedings were permitted to progress simultaneously. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 14, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTS PROGRESSIVE INSURANCE and MICHAEL COFFIELD'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answer To Complaint. Failure to comply with this Order may result in terminating sanctions and dismissal of the case upon further motion by Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID GREENE et al VS. PARK LANE ASSOCIATES LP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration (Based Upon New Facts) Of The Court'S Order Compelling Further Production
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 14, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT PARK LANE ASSOCIATES LP, Motion For Reconsideration (Based Upon New Facts) Of The Court's Order Compelling Further Production. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. The Court finds that the letter sent by Plaintiffs' counsel does not constitute a new "fact" under CCP §1008. Moreover, even if did, the rhetoric of the letter does not change the Court's prior analysis that the names of the partners are discoverable under California law. As was indicated at the last hearing, the Court will sign a confidentiality order if Defendants are concerned about the dissemination of this information. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALUMA SYSTEMS CONCRETE CONSTRUCTION OF CALIFORNIA VS. NIBBI BROSS INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 14, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANTS NIBB BROS. INC., NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC., NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Defendants' Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the First, Third, and Fourth Causes of Action. Plaintiff failed to allege how it could amend the Complaint to cure the defects complained of in the Demurrer. Maxton v. Western States Metals (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 81, 95 (burden is on the plaintiff to show that there is a reasonable possibility that the defect can be cured by amendment). The First Cause of Action alleging Breach of Contract and the Third Cause of Action for Express Indemnification fail to state facts sufficient to constitute causes of action as the exception included in the Contract's indemnity provision plainly states that the Defendants' duty to defend, indemnify, or hold harmless does not arise from acts caused by or omissions of the Plaintiff. (Complaint, Ex. A 20.) The underlying complaints in this action allege negligence as to the Plaintiff only because the employees are required to pursue worker's compensation claims against the Defendants. The acts and/or omissions for which Plaintiff seeks indemnity against arose from Plaintiff's alleged negligence and is barred by the plain language of the Contract. See C.J.L. Const., Inc. v. Univ. Plumbing (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 376, 387. The Demurrer as to the Fourth Cause of Action for Declaratory Relief is sustained without leave to amend because Plaintiff has another form of relief adequate to determine their rights as contained in the Plaintiff's Sixteenth Affirmative Defense raised against both complaints in the underlying action. Calif. Ins. Guarantee Ass'n v. Super. Ct. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 1617, 1624. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice is granted pursuant to Evidence Code § 452(d). Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is granted only as to the existence of the documents and not of the truth of the matters stated therein. See Glaski v. Bank of Am. (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1079. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: 2680 POST STREET AND 880-882 PRESIDIO AVENUE VS. JIAN MING CHEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 14, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTS JIAN CHEN, CHUN CHEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. Civ. Code sec. 4775 applies to maintenance, rather than new construction. There is no provision in Chapter 11 of the Davis-Stirling Act (the construction defect litigation chapter) that provides that a homeowner's association cannot recover relocation, loss of use, and substitute housing damages in a suit about a new construction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
